title: Water
summary: The product of every acid-base neutralization and the solvent of the whole solution course. Its molar mass is the sum of two hydrogen weights and one oxygen — the machine re-parses $\mathrm{H_2O}$ and adds the sourced atomic weights, so the 18.015 g/mol below is derived, not looked up.
notes: Bent, not linear — but shape is a Phase-2 (VSEPR) topic; here water is a mass on the balance and the net-ionic product $\mathrm{H^+ + OH^- \rightarrow H_2O}$.
